heading: Uttering counterfeit foreign bank notes
label: 483 Uttering counterfeit foreign bank notes
text: Whoever, within the United States, utters, passes, puts off, or tenders in payment, with intent to defraud, any such false, forged, altered, or counterfeited bank note or bill, mentioned in , knowing the same to be so false, forged, altered, and counterfeited, whether or not the same was made, forged, altered, or counterfeited within the United States, shall be fined under this title or imprisoned not more than 20 years, or both.section 482 of this title
notes: Historical and Revision NotesBased on title 18, U.S.C., 1940 ed., § 273 (, ).Mar. 4, 1909, ch. 321, § 15935 Stat. 1118Mandatory punishment provision was rephrased in the alternative.Changes were made in phraseology.
Amendments2001— substituted “20 years” for “one year”.Pub. L. 107–561994— substituted “fined under this title” for “fined not more than $1,000”.Pub. L. 103–322
